Ketu presides over mountain strongholds, the Pahlavas,
White Huns, Colas, Afghans, deserts, Chinese, cavemen (or border-
land), the wealthy, highly ambitious persons, energetic men, those
endowed with prowess, adulterers, quarrelsome people, those who
take pleasuse in others' difficulties, those that are puffed up with
pride, fools, unrighteous persons and those that are intent on sub-
jugating others.
[Our author refers to the White Huns in XI-61 also. Since he
uses Sveta and Sita, synonyms of white, it is clear that neither of
these words is the name of a country or people,
Gf. wa*—
